label: National Cancer Institute (NCI)
sub: Primary US federal cancer research agency — funds over $6B annually in cancer research; home of TIL therapy pioneer Steven Rosenberg

label: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center
sub: World's oldest and largest private cancer center; major site for CAR-T clinical trials, TMB biomarker research, and immunotherapy combination trials

label: MD Anderson Cancer Center
sub: Home institution of Nobel laureate James Allison; ranked #1 US cancer center; major site for immunotherapy and leukemia research

label: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute
sub: Harvard-affiliated institute; led pivotal trials in leukemia, lymphoma, and mRNA cancer vaccines; key site for KEYNOTE-522 TNBC trial

label: University of Pennsylvania — Center for Cellular Immunotherapies
sub: Birthplace of CAR-T cell therapy; Carl June's lab pioneered tisagenlecleucel (Kymriah); site of first CRISPR-edited T-cell cancer trial

label: Stanford Cancer Institute
sub: Leading AI and solid-tumor CAR-T research; Crystal Mackall's lab developing next-generation CAR-T for solid tumors; Sybil AI lung cancer risk model

label: Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center
sub: Pioneer in bone marrow transplantation and CAR-T therapy; key site for Breyanzi (lisocabtagene maraleucel) trials; conducts NCI-funded cooperative group trials

label: FDA — Center for Biologics Evaluation (CBER)
sub: Primary US regulator for CAR-T and cell/gene therapies; approved all 7 CAR-T products, TIL therapy Amtagvi, and multiple AI diagnostic tools since 2018

label: BioNTech Headquarters — Mainz, Germany
sub: Developed mRNA COVID vaccines with Pfizer; running personalized mRNA cancer vaccine program BNT111 (melanoma) and BNT113 (HPV+ head & neck cancer) in Phase 2 trials

label: Moderna Inc. — Cambridge, MA
sub: Developer of mRNA-4157/V940 personalized cancer vaccine in partnership with MSD; Phase 3 KEYNOTE-942 trial ongoing in melanoma; expanding to NSCLC and other tumors

label: German Cancer Research Center (DKFZ)
sub: Germany's largest biomedical research institution; major center for cancer epigenomics, AI pathology, and bispecific antibody development

label: Cancer Research UK Cambridge Institute
sub: Major UK centre for liquid biopsy (ctDNA) research, AI-assisted cancer diagnostics, and translational immunotherapy trials in partnership with Addenbrooke's Hospital

label: Institut Curie — Paris
sub: France's leading cancer research and treatment center; active in breast cancer ADC trials, immunotherapy, and precision oncology programs

label: European Medicines Agency (EMA)
sub: EU regulator that approved Kymriah, Yescarta, Carvykti, and other CAR-T therapies in Europe; harmonizes approval timelines with FDA on oncology breakthrough therapies

label: Karolinska Institutet — Stockholm
sub: Home institution of 2018 Nobel Committee; major research in CAR-T optimization, tumor microenvironment, and cancer genomics; hosts Tasuku Honjo's Nobel lecture

label: National Cancer Center Japan — Tokyo
sub: Japan's flagship cancer research institution; home institution of Tasuku Honjo's former collaborators; major immuno-oncology and gastric cancer research site

label: Samsung Medical Center — Seoul, South Korea
sub: Asia's largest CAR-T cell therapy program outside China; runs local manufacturing for Kymriah; active in gastric, lung, and colorectal cancer immunotherapy trials

label: Peter MacCallum Cancer Centre — Melbourne
sub: Australia's only specialist cancer hospital and research center; globally known for melanoma treatment excellence; major site for adjuvant immunotherapy trials in the Pacific

label: Istituto Nazionale dei Tumori — Milan
sub: Key ESMO 2022 DESTINY-Breast04 trial enrollment site; Italy's national cancer research institute and major European ADC and immunotherapy trial hub

label: Tata Memorial Hospital — Mumbai
sub: India's premier cancer center; treats over 75,000 new cancer patients annually; key voice on access equity in CAR-T and immunotherapy; government-supported generic oncology access program

label: INCA — National Cancer Institute Brazil
sub: Brazil's national cancer institute; conducts LATAM-wide clinical trials; expanding access to immunotherapy through SUS (public health system); major tropical oncology research hub

label: Kyoto University — PD-1 Discovery Site
sub: Laboratory of Tasuku Honjo (2018 Nobel co-laureate) where PD-1 was discovered in 1992, laying the foundation for modern checkpoint immunotherapy including nivolumab (Opdivo) and pembrolizumab (Keytruda)
